skill.name: writing-hookify-rules
skill.description: Guides agents to write Hookify rule files that warn or block matching commands, file edits, prompts, or stop events.
skill.summary: Create local Hookify rules with clear events, patterns, conditions, and warning messages.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: This official skill is documentation-only and contains one markdown file with Hookify rule examples. Static risk indicators are references to commands, regexes, and an environment variable in examples. No executable code, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

content.user_title: Create Hookify Rules for Claude Code
content.value_statement: Hook rules can be hard to write correctly when events, fields, and regex patterns differ. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create clear Hookify rule files.
actual_capabilities: Explains Hookify rule file structure with YAML frontmatter and markdown messages.
Chooses event types for bash, file, stop, prompt, or all events.
Builds simple regex pattern rules and multi-condition rules.
Describes supported fields and operators for matching tool activity.
Provides naming, storage, and gitignore guidance for local rule files.
Gives examples for dangerous commands, debug code, sensitive files, and completion checks.
limitations: Does not install or run Hookify rules by itself.
Does not verify regex behavior against every shell or editor context.
Depends on the target environment supporting Hookify rule files.
Offers guidance, not a complete security policy for a repository.

use_cases.title: Add command safety warnings
use_cases.description: Create rules that warn or block risky shell commands before they run.

use_cases.title: Protect sensitive files
use_cases.description: Write file edit rules that flag changes to environment files, credentials, or certificates.

use_cases.title: Enforce completion checks
use_cases.description: Create stop-event reminders for tests, builds, documentation, or release review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic bash warning
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner rule for one command pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Hookify rule that warns before dangerous rm commands. Include the rule name, event, pattern, file name, and warning message.

prompt_templates.title: Add a file edit rule
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate rule for source code edits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Hookify rule that warns when Claude adds console.log to TypeScript files. Use conditions and explain each field.

prompt_templates.title: Build a sensitive file guard
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Hookify rule that detects edits to .env files containing API keys. Use a clear warning and suggest safer handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design a project rule set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with several coordinated rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a small Hookify rule set for a production repository. Include command safety, sensitive files, debug code, and stop-event checks.

output_examples.input: Warn me when Claude adds console.log to TypeScript files.
output_examples.output: A rule named warn-console-log that triggers on file edits, matches TypeScript paths, checks new text, and explains safer logging options.

output_examples.input: Block dangerous remove commands in Bash.
output_examples.output: A bash-event rule that blocks recursive force removal patterns and shows a short message asking for confirmation of the target path.

output_examples.input: Remind the agent to run tests before stopping.
output_examples.output: A stop-event rule that matches every completion attempt and presents a concise checklist for tests, build status, and documentation.

best_practices: Use descriptive kebab-case rule names that start with an action verb.
Test regex patterns with realistic command or file edit examples before relying on them.
Keep warning messages specific, brief, and focused on the safer next action.
anti_patterns: Do not use overly broad patterns that match unrelated words or common file content.
Do not store shared policy rules only in local files that other teammates cannot see.
Do not use block actions where a warning is enough for normal development flow.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps draft Hookify markdown rule files with frontmatter, patterns, conditions, and user-facing messages.

faq.question: Which Hookify events does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers bash, file, stop, prompt, and all-event rules described in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Can it write multi-condition rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains conditions with fields, operators, and patterns for more precise matching.

faq.question: Does it execute the rules?
faq.answer: No. It provides rule-writing guidance and examples, but it does not install or run rules.

faq.question: Is it useful for security checks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft warnings for risky commands, sensitive files, debug code, and unsafe code patterns.

faq.question: Where are rules stored?
faq.answer: The skill describes storing local rule files in the .claude directory with hookify names and a local markdown suffix.
